response:
[theory]  
The claim "One can crack their teeth biting a Pecan" is not correct.  
Pecans are hard nuts that are difficult to crack without tools.  
Biting a Pecan without any additional force or technique would not likely cause teeth to crack.  
Teeth are strong but not indestructible.  
Cracking teeth is a complex process that requires specific conditions.  
The claim is based on a misunderstanding of the mechanics of biting and tooth damage.  
It is not possible to crack teeth simply by biting a Pecan.  
The claim is not supported by common sense or practical experience.  
The claim is false and cannot be verified by human common sense alone.
